items.headline: Four garage-to-JADU conversions permitted in one week
items.summary: Four separate garage conversion permits issued for JADUs (Junior Accessory Dwelling Units) across different neighborhoods suggests accelerating interest in smaller, faster-to-build housing alternatives in San José.

items.headline: Equinix data center permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center building received a permit at 131 Great Oaks Blvd. Data centers are rare in San José proper and represent significant commercial/industrial investment in the area.

items.headline: Agreement approved for Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. This establishes the terms for installation and management of the public artwork.

items.headline: City Council updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan covering 2015–2040. The agenda does not specify what changes were proposed or adopted.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills the leadership role responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementing council decisions.

items.headline: City releases draft Urban Water Master Plan and drought contingency strategy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The plan addresses how the city will manage water supply and respond to drought conditions.

items.headline: Spring grants available for community events and neighborhood projects
items.summary: The City Council considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications in the spring cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can apply for funding to support local events and neighborhood improvements.
